engineIntelligence.h2: MG 4 EV Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main electric drive units fitted to the MG 4 EV in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt motor costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: MG 4 EV TZ180XS0951 motor - electric 64kWh replacement UK
knownFailures: MCU failure - sudden power loss or no acceleration, often requires control unit replacement.
Motor whining noise - bearing wear or inverter issues developing after 40k-60k miles.

cards.altText: MG 4 EV TZ180XS1001 motor - electric 43kWh replacement UK
knownFailures: Charging system faults - DC fast charging failure linked to motor control communication errors.
Reduced power mode - thermal management issues triggering limp mode on high-mileage units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt motor: £2,400-£3,900 (supply only) Estimated from comparable TZ180XS units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: MG 4 EV XPower dual motor - electric 64kWh replacement UK specialist
knownFailures: Front motor failure - one motor fails while other remains operational, requiring dual-unit assessment.
Combined torque system fault - software calibration issues causing uneven power distribution.
cards.specs: Electric · 64kWh · 320kW (429hp) · Dual Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors

commonProblems.h2: MG 4 EV Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The MG 4 EV's electric powertrain is generally reliable, but MCU failures, motor bearing wear, and charging system faults can drive replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt motor supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: MCU Failure - Why MG 4 EV Motors Lose Power Suddenly
problems.affectedModels: MG 4 EV Long Range, Trophy, Essence (2023-2024, TZ180XS0951)
problems.rootCause: Motor Control Unit software corruption or hardware fault interrupts power delivery from battery to motor.

repairOptions.tier: MCU Software Reset/Reflash - Diagnostic reset of motor control software
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD diagnostic session, software update, system recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; suitable if fault is software-only, not hardware

repairOptions.tier: MCU Replacement Only - Swap control unit, retain existing motor
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty MCU, install refurbished/rebuilt unit, programming
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if motor windings and bearings are confirmed healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Motor + MCU - Complete drive unit swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt motor assembly with integrated MCU, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; addresses root cause comprehensively

problems.recommendation: If your MG 4 EV is under the 7-year/80k-mile manufacturer warranty, contact MG first. For out-of-warranty vehicles over 50k miles, a full rebuilt motor often offers better long-term value than MCU-only repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TZ180XS0951 motor prices for your MG 4 EV

problems.h4: Motor Bearing Wear - Whining Noise Development
problems.affectedModels: MG 4 EV Long Range, SE, Extended Range (TZ180XS0951, TZ180XS1001)
problems.rootCause: High-speed rotor bearings degrade from continuous load, causing audible whine that worsens with acceleration.

repairOptions.tier: Bearing Inspection Only - Diagnostic assessment of motor noise
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Motor removal, bearing inspection, noise measurement report
repairOptions.longevity: Identifies severity; does not fix the problem

repairOptions.tier: Bearing Replacement Service - Replace worn bearings, retain motor
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Motor disassembly, bearing press-fit replacement, reassembly, testing
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if windings and rotor are undamaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Motor - Complete unit swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt motor with upgraded bearings, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for motors over 60k miles or with additional faults

problems.recommendation: Bearing noise rarely improves with time. If your MG 4 EV has over 50k miles, investing in a rebuilt motor with upgraded bearings typically delivers better reliability than a bearing-only repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG 4 EV motor bearing replacement specialists

problems.h4: Charging System Faults - DC Fast Charging Failure
problems.affectedModels: MG 4 EV Urban, SE, Long Range (all variants, TZ180XS series)
problems.rootCause: Communication error between onboard charger, battery management system, and motor control unit interrupts DC charging protocol.

repairOptions.tier: Diagnostic Scan & Reset - System reset and fault code clearance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD diagnostic session, fault code analysis, system reset
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for intermittent software glitches only

repairOptions.tier: Onboard Charger Replacement - Replace charging module only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty charger unit, install refurbished unit, programming
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if motor and battery are confirmed healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Motor + Charger Integration
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt motor assembly with integrated charging compatibility
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vehicles or multiple system faults

problems.recommendation: Charging faults can stem from multiple components. Get a full diagnostic before committing to repairs. If your MG 4 EV has high mileage, a rebuilt motor with integrated charging compatibility often proves more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare MG 4 EV charging fault repair vs motor replacement costs

problems.h4: XPower Dual Motor Imbalance - Performance Variant Specific
problems.rootCause: Uneven thermal loading or software calibration drift causes one motor to underperform, triggering torque vectoring errors.

repairOptions.tier: Software Recalibration - Reset torque distribution parameters
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Specialist diagnostic session, software update, performance testing
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage calibration drift only

repairOptions.tier: Single Motor Replacement - Replace faulty front or rear unit only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty motor, install rebuilt unit, dual-motor calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if second motor is confirmed healthy via diagnostics

repairOptions.tier: Dual Motor Replacement - Rebuilt Pair - Complete AWD system rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit both rebuilt motors, integrated calibration, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for XPower over 40k miles or with multiple faults

problems.recommendation: XPower's performance demands place unique stress on both motors. If one motor has failed, the other is likely nearing end-of-life. For vehicles over 30k miles, replacing both motors together often delivers better value and reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG 4 EV XPower dual motor replacement specialists

problems.group: Don't Let Motor Failure Write Off Your MG 4 EV
problems.h4: Don't Let Motor Failure Write Off Your MG 4 EV
problems.cta: Compare MG 4 EV Engine Replacement Prices Now →

finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG 4 EV and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: MG 4 EV Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace an MG 4 EV motor?
items.answer: Rebuilt MG 4 EV motors typically cost £2,400-£4,500 supply-only, depending on variant and motor code. XPower dual-motor replacements range £5,200-£8,500. Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 labour.
keyPoints: Long Range TZ180XS0951: £2,800-£4,500 rebuilt supply-only
Urban TZ180XS1001: £2,400-£3,900 rebuilt supply-only
XPower dual motor: £5,200-£8,500 rebuilt supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £800-£1,500
Prices vary by supplier, location and motor condition
items.cta: Compare rebuilt MG 4 EV motor prices for your variant

items.question: What are the most common MG 4 EV motor problems?
items.answer: MCU failure causing sudden power loss, motor bearing wear creating whining noise, and DC charging communication faults are the most reported MG 4 EV motor issues.
keyPoints: MCU faults trigger "no acceleration" or limp mode on TZ180XS0951 units
Bearing wear develops audible whine after 40k-60k miles
DC charging errors linked to motor control communication on Urban variants
XPower dual-motor calibration issues cause uneven power distribution
items.cta: Get quotes for MG 4 EV motor fault diagnosis specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing an MG 4 EV motor or buying a new car?
items.answer: For MG 4 EVs under 70k miles and valued over £15k, motor replacement is usually cost-effective versus purchasing new, especially with 12-month warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt motor costs £2,400-£4,500 versus £25k+ for new MG 4 EV
Manufacturer warranty (7 years/80k miles) may cover replacement
High-mileage vehicles over 80k miles may favour replacement investment
XPower performance variants retain strong value, favouring repair
items.cta: Find out if MG 4 EV motor replacement makes financial sense

items.question: Can any garage fit an MG 4 EV replacement motor?
items.answer: Only garages with EV high-voltage certification and MG-specific diagnostic equipment should fit MG 4 EV motors due to safety and calibration requirements.
keyPoints: High-voltage systems require certified EV technician training
MG-specific software calibration needed post-installation
Incorrect fitting can void remaining manufacturer warranty
EngineMarket's network includes only vetted EV-specialist garages
items.cta: Find MG 4 EV motor supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Does replacing the MG 4 EV motor affect the manufacturer warranty?
items.answer: Using MG-approved rebuilt motors and certified specialists preserves remaining 7-year/80k-mile warranty; non-approved parts or fitters may void coverage.
keyPoints: MG's 7-year/80,000-mile warranty may cover motor replacement if in period
Non-MG-approved parts can void remaining warranty coverage
Keep full documentation of replacement for warranty claims
EngineMarket suppliers can provide MG-compatible warranty options
items.cta: Check MG 4 EV motor warranty compatibility before ordering

items.question: How long does MG 4 EV motor replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only motors typically dispatch within 1-3 days; full supply & fit service takes 2-5 days depending on specialist availability and calibration requirements.
keyPoints: Rebuilt motors often available for next-day dispatch from UK stock
Specialist fitting requires 1-2 days for removal, installation, calibration
Software updates and testing add half-day to completion time
XPower dual-motor replacements may require additional calibration time
items.cta: Get fast quotes for MG 4 EV motor replacement with delivery times

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt MG 4 EV motors?
items.answer: Reconditioned MG 4 EV motors receive cleaning and worn-part replacement; rebuilt units undergo comprehensive overhaul with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cleaned, tested, minor parts replaced, basic warranty
Rebuilt: full disassembly, upgraded bearings/seals, MCU calibration, 12-month warranty
Rebuilt costs £300-£800 more but offers better longevity
Rebuilt recommended for motors over 50k miles or with multiple faults
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt MG 4 EV motor options

items.question: Can I buy a used MG 4 EV motor and fit it myself?
items.answer: While possible, DIY MG 4 EV motor replacement is strongly discouraged due to high-voltage safety risks, specialist calibration requirements, and potential warranty implications.
keyPoints: High-voltage systems pose serious electrocution risk without proper training
MG-specific software calibration requires dealer-level diagnostic tools
Incorrect installation can damage battery, MCU or other drivetrain components
Professional fitting ensures warranty validity and post-installation support
items.cta: Get quotes for professional MG 4 EV motor supply & fit services

items.question: How do I find my MG 4 EV motor code?
items.answer: Your MG 4 EV motor code (TZ180XS0951, TZ180XS1001, etc.) is on the motor housing, in the vehicle handbook, or retrievable via registration lookup through EngineMarket.
keyPoints: Motor code stamped on drive unit housing near mounting points
Vehicle handbook or service records list original motor specification
DVLA registration lookup instantly identifies variant and motor code
Engine code essential for ordering correct replacement unit
items.cta: Identify your MG 4 EV motor code instantly with registration lookup

items.question: Are rebuilt MG 4 EV motors reliable?
items.answer: Yes - rebuilt MG 4 EV motors from EngineMarket suppliers include upgraded components addressing original failure points, bench testing, and minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units replace worn bearings, seals and sensors with upgraded parts
MCU calibration updated to latest software version during rebuild
Bench testing verifies performance before dispatch to customer
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides post-installation confidence
